chunks.text: Stereo. HC JD A 38. Judgment Sheet IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, BAHAWALPUR BENCH, BAHAWALPUR. JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT Murder Reference No.10 of 2019 (The State Vs. Abdul Rehman) Criminal Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 (Abdul Rehman Vs. The State.) J U D G M E N T Date of hearing: 16.11.2021. Appellant by: Ms. Tania Bazai, Advocate (learned defence counsel appointed on State expense). State by: Ch. Asghar Ali Gill, Deputy Prosecutor General. SADIQ MAHMUD KHURRAM, J.– Abdul Rehman son of Ahmad Din (convict) was tried by the learned Additional Sessions Judge, Rahim Yar Khan in the case FIR No. 344 of 2018 dated 17.11.2018 registered at Police Station Saddar Rahim Yar Khan, District Rahim Yar Khan in respect of an offence under section 302 PPC for committing the Qatl-i-Amd of Mst Naseem Bibi daughter of Qadir Bakhsh (deceased). The learned trial court vide judgment dated 27.05.2019, convicted Abdul Rehman son of Ahmad Din (convict) and sentenced him as infra: Abdul Rehman son of Ahmad Din: Death under section 302(b) PPC as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Mst Naseem Bibi daughter of Qadir Bakhsh (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.300,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of the deceased and in case of Murder Reference No. 10 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 2 default thereof, the convict was directed to further undergo six months of simple imprisonment. The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. 2. Feeling aggrieved, Abdul Rehman son of Ahmad Din (convict) lodged Criminal Appeal No.353-J of 2019 through jail assailing his conviction and sentence. The learned trial court submitted Murder Reference No.10 of 2019 under section 374 Cr.P.C. seeking confirmation or otherwise of the sentence of death awarded to the appellant namely Abdul Rehman son of Ahmad Din.

Feeling aggrieved, Abdul Rehman son of Ahmad Din (convict) lodged Criminal Appeal No.353-J of 2019 through jail assailing his conviction and sentence. The learned trial court submitted Murder Reference No.10 of 2019 under section 374 Cr.P.C. seeking confirmation or otherwise of the sentence of death awarded to the appellant namely Abdul Rehman son of Ahmad Din. We intend to dispose of the Criminal Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 and Murder Reference No.10 of 2019 through this single judgment. 3. Precisely, the necessary facts of the prosecution case, as narrated by Abdul Majeed (PW-8), the complainant of the case are as under:- “Stated that my sister Mst. Naseem Bibi deceased aged about 40/41 years was married with Abdul Rahman accused present in the court. Out of the wedlock five daughters and two sons born. Abdul Rahman accused oftenly used to quarrel with my sister Mst. Naseem Bibi. On 16.11.2018 at evening time, Abdul Rahman accused also abused my sister, due to that, after Isha prayer time, after taking meal, I alongwith Mukhtiar Ahmad my brother and Hameed Ashraf nephew PWs went to the house of Abdul Rahman. We asked the Abdul Rahman accused present in the court not to torture our sister Mst. Naseem Bibi and till night we remained busy in talking with each other. Thereafter, I alongwith Mukhtiar Ahmad and Hameed Ashraf slept in the house of Abdul Rahman in "Chapra" like room. Abdul Rahman also slept in the room with wife and children. At about 12:30 (mid night), my sister Naseem Bibi raised hue and cry, whereupon, I alongwith 'Mukhtiar Ahmad and Hameed Ashraf PWs Murder Reference No. 10 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 3 attracted to the spot/room and saw Abdul Rahman accused was inflicting repeatedly hatchet blows on the body of my sister Naseem Bibi. We tried to rescue my sister whereas Abdul Rahman extended threats to us for dire consequences and succeeded to flee away from spot alongwith hatchet. I alongwith witnesses attended Naseem Bibi, who succumbed to the injuries at the spot.

We tried to rescue my sister whereas Abdul Rahman extended threats to us for dire consequences and succeeded to flee away from spot alongwith hatchet. I alongwith witnesses attended Naseem Bibi, who succumbed to the injuries at the spot. Accused Abdul Rahman by inflicting right and blunt side of hatchet blows committed the murder of my sister Mst. Naseem Bibi. After receiving the information, police reached there, I got recorded my statement Ex-P-D, which bears my thumb impression.” 4. After the formal investigation of the case report under section 173 of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 was submitted before the learned trial court wherein the appellant namely Abdul Rehman son of Ahmad Din was sent to face trial. The learned trial court framed the charge against the accused on 19.03.2019, to which the accused pleaded not guilty and claimed trial. 5. The prosecution in order to prove its case got statements of as many as ten witnesses recorded. The ocular account of the case was furnished by Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9). Ijaz Ahmad 193/C (PW-1) stated that on 21.11.2018 Ghulam Awais 1228/C handed over to him five photographs (P-1 to P-5) of the dead body of the deceased. Ghulam Muhammad 124/C (PW-2) stated that on 17.11.2018 he escorted the dead body of the deceased to the hospital and received the last worn clothes of the deceased from the Woman Medical Officer after the post mortem examination. Shaukat Ali, ASI (PW-3) stated that on 17.11.2018 he went to the place of occurrence and recorded the oral statement (Exh.PD) of Abdul Majeed (PW-8). Faryad Ali Patwari (PW-4) stated that Murder Reference No. 10 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 4 he prepared the scaled site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.PE). Muhammad Umar (PW-5) stated that on 21.11.2018 he developed the photographs (P-1 to P-5). Zeeshan Akram 1863/HC (PW-6) stated that on 17.11.2018 Ghulam Abbas, SI (PW-10) handed over to him the articles taken by him into possession from the place of occurrence and on 30.11.2018, Ghulam Abbas, SI (PW-10) handed over to him a sealed parcel said to contain a hatchet and on 13.12.2018, he handed over the sealed parcels said to contain the blood stained earth, vaginal swabs of the deceased and the hatchet to Ghulam Abbas, SI (PW-10) for their onward transmission to the office of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore.

Muhammad Umar (PW-5) stated that on 21.11.2018 he developed the photographs (P-1 to P-5). Zeeshan Akram 1863/HC (PW-6) stated that on 17.11.2018 Ghulam Abbas, SI (PW-10) handed over to him the articles taken by him into possession from the place of occurrence and on 30.11.2018, Ghulam Abbas, SI (PW-10) handed over to him a sealed parcel said to contain a hatchet and on 13.12.2018, he handed over the sealed parcels said to contain the blood stained earth, vaginal swabs of the deceased and the hatchet to Ghulam Abbas, SI (PW-10) for their onward transmission to the office of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore. Ghulam Abbas, SI (PW-10) investigated the case from 17.11.2018 till 21.03.2019, arrested the appellant on 27.11.2018, and detailed the facts of the investigation as conducted by him in his statement before the learned trial court. 6. The prosecution also got Dr. Maryam Mumtaz (PW-7) examined, who on 17.11.2018 was posted as Woman Medical Officer at Sheikh Zayed Hospital, Rahim Yar Khan and on the same day conducted the postmortem examination of the dead body of Mst Naseem Bibi daughter of Qadir Bakhsh (deceased). Dr. Maryam Mumtaz (PW-7),on examining the dead body of Mst Naseem Bibi daughter of Qadir Bakhsh (deceased) observed as under:- “Description of injuries: Injury No. 1. A lacerated wound measuring 2.5 X 1.5 cm approaching to the skull bone situated on the right lateral side of forehead 2cm above the lateral side of right eyebrow. Injury No.2. There is blackish coloured bruise in right orbit extending 3 cm below to right cheek. Murder Reference No. 10 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 5 Injury No.3. An incised wound of 4X0.3cm situated on the interior surface of chin more toward left side, horizontally lying, 3cm below left angle of mouth. Injury No.4. A lacerated wound measuring 3.5X1.5cm situated on right temporal part of head, 6cm above the pinna of right ear. On deep dissection, Skull bone was fractured and haematoma of 3X4cm intracranial cavity. Injury No.5. A lacerated wound of 6X3cm situated in the middle of occipital part of skull extending to right occipital region, 7 cm below and posterior to injury No.4. On deep dissection. Skull bone is fractured and a massive heamatoma is present in intracranial cavity.

On deep dissection. Skull bone is fractured and a massive heamatoma is present in intracranial cavity. Injury No.6 Spindle shape lacerated wound of 7X3cm situated on posterior surface of head in occipital region slightly towards left side, 10cm posterior to pinna of left ear. On deep dissection. Skull bone is fractured and a massive heamatoma is present in intracranial cavity. Injury No.7. A lacerated wound measuring 3X1cm situated on top of head, slightly towards posterior region, 2cm above injury No.6....................... Opinion. After conducting thorough autopsy, I was of the opinion that in this case, cause of death is injury No.4,5 and 6 which fractured the skull bone, damaged brain matter, are sufficient cause of death in normal course of life, Injuries were caused by blunt weapon except Injury No:3 which is caused by sharp edge weapon. I issued the Postmortem examination report Ex P-F. which is in my hand and bears my signatures and seals. Diagram is Ex P-F/1. 1 also endorsed the inquest report Ex.P- C/1 and injury statement Ex P-C. Probable time that lapsed between injury and death within half an hour. Between death and postmortem less than 10 hours.” 7. On 16.4.2018 the learned Assistant District Public Prosecutor gave up the prosecution witness namely Ghous Bakhsh 1849-C as being unnecessary. On 11.05.2018 the learned Assistant District Public Prosecutor gave up the prosecution witness namely Mukhtiar Ahmed as being unnecessary. On 14.05.2018, the learned Deputy District Public Murder Reference No. 10 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 6 Prosecutor closed the prosecution evidence after tendering in evidence the reports of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PK and Exh.PM). 8. After the closure of prosecution evidence, the learned trial court examined the appellant namely Abdul Rehman son of Ahmad Din under section 342 Cr.P.C. and in answer to the question why this case against you and why the PWs have deposed against you, he replied that he had been involved in the case falsely and was innocent.

Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 6 Prosecutor closed the prosecution evidence after tendering in evidence the reports of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PK and Exh.PM). 8. After the closure of prosecution evidence, the learned trial court examined the appellant namely Abdul Rehman son of Ahmad Din under section 342 Cr.P.C. and in answer to the question why this case against you and why the PWs have deposed against you, he replied that he had been involved in the case falsely and was innocent. The appellant namely Abdul Rehman son of Ahmad Din opted not to get himself examined under section 340 Cr.P.C. and did not adduce any evidence in his defence. 9. On the conclusion of the trial, the learned Additional Sessions Judge, Rahim Yar Khan convicted and sentenced the appellant as referred to above. 10. The contention of the learned counsel for the appellant precisely is that the whole case is fabricated and false and the prosecution remained unable to prove the facts in issue and did not produce any unimpeachable, admissible, and relevant evidence. Learned counsel for the appellant further contended that the story of the prosecution mentioned in the statements of the witnesses, on the face of it, is highly improbable. Learned counsel for the appellant further contended that the statements of the prosecution witnesses were not worthy of any reliance. The learned counsel for the appellant also submitted that the recovery of the hatchet (P-11) was full of procedural defects, of no legal worth and value, and result of fake proceedings. The learned counsel for the appellant also Murder Reference No. 10 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 7 argued that the appellant had been involved in the occurrence due to enmity. The learned counsel for the appellant finally submitted that the prosecution has totally failed to prove the case against the accused beyond the shadow of a doubt. 11. On the other hand, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General contended that the prosecution has proved its case beyond the shadow of a doubt by producing independent witnesses. The learned Deputy Prosecutor General further argued that the deceased died as a result of injuries suffered at the hands of the appellant.

On the other hand, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General contended that the prosecution has proved its case beyond the shadow of a doubt by producing independent witnesses. The learned Deputy Prosecutor General further argued that the deceased died as a result of injuries suffered at the hands of the appellant. The learned Deputy Prosecutor General further contended that the medical evidence also corroborated the statements of Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9). The learned Deputy Prosecutor General further argued that the recovery of the hatchet (P-11) from the appellant also corroborated the ocular account. The learned Deputy Prosecutor General contended that there was no occasion for the prosecution witnesses, who were related to the deceased, to substitute the real offender with the innocent in this case. Lastly, they prayed for the rejection of the appeal. 12. We have heard the learned counsel for the appellant, the learned counsel for the complainant, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General and with their able assistance perused the record and evidence recorded during the trial., 13. The whole prosecution case revolves around the statements of Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9), the eye witnesses of the occurrence. The relationship of both the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) with the deceased is Murder Reference No. 10 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 8 on record. Mst Naseem Bibi (deceased) was the real sister of Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and the real maternal aunt of Hameed Ashraf (PW-9). Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) were admittedly not the residents of the place of occurrence. According to both the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) they had their own separate houses in the same village. Abdul Majeed (PW-8) during cross-examination stated as under:- “Distance between my house and house of Abdul Rehman is six acres................

According to both the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) they had their own separate houses in the same village. Abdul Majeed (PW-8) during cross-examination stated as under:- “Distance between my house and house of Abdul Rehman is six acres................ The houses of Hameed Ashraf and Mukhtar PWs are situated adjacent to my house. ” It is an admitted fact that both the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) were not the residents of anywhere near the place of occurrence rather they were residing in their own houses which were at a distance of about six acres from the place of occurrence. We have also perused the scaled site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.PE) as prepared by Faryad Ali Patwari (PW-4) and the rough site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.PJ) as prepared by Ghulam Abbas, SI (PW-10), the Investigating Officer of the case, and find that neither the houses nor any place of work nor any land of the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) have been shown in the said site plans (Exh.PE and Exh.PJ). In this manner, both the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW- 8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) can be validly termed as “chance Murder Reference No. 10 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 9 witnesses” and therefore were under a bounden duty to provide a convincing reason for their presence at the place of occurrence, at the time of occurrence and were also under a duty to prove their presence by producing some physical proof of the same. In order to prove their presence at the place of occurrence, at the time of occurrence, the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) claimed that the appellant had abused Mst. Naseem Bibi (deceased) and therefore the witnesses had proceeded to the house of the appellant to resolve the dispute between the spouses.

In order to prove their presence at the place of occurrence, at the time of occurrence, the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) claimed that the appellant had abused Mst. Naseem Bibi (deceased) and therefore the witnesses had proceeded to the house of the appellant to resolve the dispute between the spouses. What we have noticed and what has raised our concern is the fact that both the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) made contradictory statements as to the reason for them to proceed to the place of occurrence. According to Abdul Majeed (PW-8), Mst. Naseem Bibi (deceased) had told him that the appellant had a fight with her sometime in the morning and thereafter Abdul Majeed (PW-8) continued performing his work, however, later in the evening told the other witnesses of the said fact whereas according to the statement of Hameed Ashraf (PW-9), the altercation between the appellant and Mst. Naseem Bibi (deceased) had taken place in the evening. Abdul Majeed (PW-8) during cross-examination stated as under:- “I am street vendor and was present in the Basti near to the house of my sister on 16.11.2018 by that time and she told me about the quarrel and I asked her to go to home as I would come there in evening. On 16.11.2018 I remained busy in selling the grocery items at day time. I have separate house living alongwith my wife. The houses of Hameed Ashraf and Mukhtiar PWs are situated adjacent to my house. On their Murder Reference No. 10 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 10 arrival at their houses, I told them about the quarrel of my sister and Abdul Rahman and thereafter, we after taking meal at Isha prayer time, went to the house of Abdul Rahman accused.” (emphasis supplied) The above referred portion of the cross-examination of Abdul Majeed (PW-8) clearly reflects that Mst. Naseem Bibi (deceased) had a meeting with Abdul Majeed (PW-8) in the morning and Abdul Majeed (PW-8) told her that he would come to her house in the evening.

Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 10 arrival at their houses, I told them about the quarrel of my sister and Abdul Rahman and thereafter, we after taking meal at Isha prayer time, went to the house of Abdul Rahman accused.” (emphasis supplied) The above referred portion of the cross-examination of Abdul Majeed (PW-8) clearly reflects that Mst. Naseem Bibi (deceased) had a meeting with Abdul Majeed (PW-8) in the morning and Abdul Majeed (PW-8) told her that he would come to her house in the evening. Contrary to this statement of Abdul Majeed (PW-8), the other prosecution witness namely Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) stated in his statement before the learned trial court that Mst. Naseem Bibi (deceased) had an altercation with the appellant in the evening on 16.11.2018. Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) in his statement before the learned trial court stated as under:- “Stated that on 16.11.2018 at evening time, Abdul Rahman accused abused Mst. Naseem Bibi, due to that, after Isha prayer time, after taking meal, I alongwith Mukhtiar Ahmad and Abdul Majeed went to the house of Abdul Rahman. ” (emphasis supplied) We have also noticed that not only both the prosecution witnesses remained inconsistent as to the time when Mst. Naseem Bibi (deceased) was abused by the appellant but also remained inconsistent as to the source through which they came to know about the said altercation between Mst. Naseem Bibi (deceased) and the appellant. According to Abdul Majeed (PW-8) he was told of the altercation by Mst. Naseem Bibi (deceased) in the morning whereas the other prosecution witness namely Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) did not mention anything regarding his source of the knowledge of the altercation between Mst. Naseem Bibi (deceased) Murder Reference No. 10 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 11 and the appellant. Both the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) remained unable to give consistent evidence for the reason which necessitated their proceeding to the place of occurrence and remained a purpose unproved, entailing failure of the prosecution witnesses to prove their reasons for their departure to the place of occurrence.

Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 11 and the appellant. Both the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) remained unable to give consistent evidence for the reason which necessitated their proceeding to the place of occurrence and remained a purpose unproved, entailing failure of the prosecution witnesses to prove their reasons for their departure to the place of occurrence. We have also noted that despite the fact that both the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) were married and had their own houses, situated just at a distance of about nine acres from the place of occurrence, but still, instead of returning to their own houses, preferred to sleep at the place of occurrence, without there being any reason for them to do so. Abdul Majeed (PW-8) during cross-examination stated as under:- “I have separate house living along with my wife............... Hameed and Mukhtiar PWs are married person” Not only both the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) failed to explain their reasons for sleeping at the place of occurrence but also admitted that it was not a usual practice of theirs. More importantly both the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) admitted that prior to the occurrence they had never ever slept in the house of the appellant ever before. Abdul Majeed (PW-8) during cross-examination stated as under:- “I never went to sleep in the house of Abdul Rahman accused prior to 16.11.2018.” Murder Reference No. 10 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 12 Similarly, Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) also admitted during cross-examination as under:- “I never used to sleep in the house of my aunt prior to the occurrence. ” We have also noted with concern that both the prosecution witnesses admitted that at the time of their arrival at the house of the appellant, there was no altercation between Mst. Naseem Bibi (deceased) and the appellant and both the witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) also admitted that the dispute between the spouses had been resolved prior to the witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) proceeding to sleep at the place of occurrence.

Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 12 Similarly, Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) also admitted during cross-examination as under:- “I never used to sleep in the house of my aunt prior to the occurrence. ” We have also noted with concern that both the prosecution witnesses admitted that at the time of their arrival at the house of the appellant, there was no altercation between Mst. Naseem Bibi (deceased) and the appellant and both the witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) also admitted that the dispute between the spouses had been resolved prior to the witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) proceeding to sleep at the place of occurrence. When the dispute between the spouses had been resolved and when both the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) were married men with their own families residing with them in their houses situated near to the place of occurrence and when both the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) had never ever slept inside the matrimonial house of Mst. Naseem Bibi (deceased) ever before,then their claim that on 16.11.2018 they found it necessary to sleep at the place of occurrence cannot be believed. Furthermore, according to the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) they had slept at the place of occurrence, however the Investigating Officer of the case did not observe or took into possession any beddings or any cots from the place of occurrence which were used by the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) to sleep on. Ghulam Abbas, SI (PW-10), the Investigating Officer of the case, visited Murder Reference No. 10 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 13 the place of occurrence, after the occurrence and remained there for a considerable time. During the course of his stay at the place of occurrence, Ghulam Abbas, SI (PW-10), the Investigating Officer of the case, did not take into possession the beddings or any cots from the place of occurrence which were used by the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) alongwith the other recoveries though there was no occasion for the said beddings or the cots, which were allegedly used by the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) to sleep at the place of occurrence, not to have been present at the place of occurrence or not being taken into possession by the Investigating Officer or not being observed as present during his visit at the place of occurrence, if the same were available.

Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 13 the place of occurrence, after the occurrence and remained there for a considerable time. During the course of his stay at the place of occurrence, Ghulam Abbas, SI (PW-10), the Investigating Officer of the case, did not take into possession the beddings or any cots from the place of occurrence which were used by the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) alongwith the other recoveries though there was no occasion for the said beddings or the cots, which were allegedly used by the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) to sleep at the place of occurrence, not to have been present at the place of occurrence or not being taken into possession by the Investigating Officer or not being observed as present during his visit at the place of occurrence, if the same were available. The non-availability of any beddings or cots at the place of occurrence used by the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) and the failure of the complainant of the case as well as the Investigating Officer of the case to produce the same before the learned trial court leads to only one conclusion and that being that no such beddings or cots were available at the place of occurrence which could have been used by the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) for sleeping on. We have also perused the scaled site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.PE) as prepared by Faryad Ali Patwari (PW-4) and the rough site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.PJ) as prepared by Ghulam Abbas, SI (PW-10), the Investigating Officer of the case, and find that no cots used by the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) to sleep at the place of occurrence have been shown in the Murder Reference No. 10 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 14 said site plans (Exh.PE and Exh.PJ). Furthermore, during cross- examination Faryad Ali, Patwari (PW-4) admitted that even the names of the prosecution witnesses were not mentioned in the scaled site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.PE). In this manner, both the eye witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) failed miserably to establish the reason for their presence and their presence at the place of occurrence, at the time of occurrence.The prosecution was under a bounden duty to establish that the occurrence had indeed taken place when the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) had proceeded to, arrived and were sleeping at the place of the occurrence and the failure to prove the said facts has vitiated our trust in Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9).

Furthermore, during cross- examination Faryad Ali, Patwari (PW-4) admitted that even the names of the prosecution witnesses were not mentioned in the scaled site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.PE). In this manner, both the eye witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) failed miserably to establish the reason for their presence and their presence at the place of occurrence, at the time of occurrence.The prosecution was under a bounden duty to establish that the occurrence had indeed taken place when the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) had proceeded to, arrived and were sleeping at the place of the occurrence and the failure to prove the said facts has vitiated our trust in Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9). In this respect, reliance is placed on the case of “Muhammad Rafiq v. State” (2014 SCMR 1698) wherein the august Supreme Court of Pakistan rejected the claim of witnesses who lived one kilometer away from the occurrence, but on the day of occurrence stated to be present near the spot as they working as labourers, inasmuch as they failed to give any detail of the projects they were working on. Reliance is also placed on the case of “Usman alias Kaloo v. State” (2017 SCMR 622) wherein the august Supreme Court of Pakistan held that the ocular account of the incident had been furnished by Zahoor Ahmad complainant Ghulam Farid and Manzoor Ahmed witnesses in the said case who were all residents of some other houses and they were not the inmates of the house wherein the occurrence had taken place and therefore the said eye-witnesses were, thus, chance witnesses and declared not worthy of reliance. Reliance is also placed on the case of “Nasrullah alias Nasro v. The State” (2017 Murder Reference No. 10 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 15 SCMR 724).All these omissions as detailed above are conspicuous by their absence. In absence of physical proof or the reason for the presence of the witnesses at the crime scene, the same cannot be believed. 14. We have also observed that according to the witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9), the occurrence took place only when they had arrived at the place of occurrence and were sleeping there. This narrative of the witnesses that the accused, despite observant of the fact that the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9), for the first time in their lives, were sleeping inside the house where the occurrence took place, still committed the same, is atypical and cannot be believed.

We have also observed that according to the witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9), the occurrence took place only when they had arrived at the place of occurrence and were sleeping there. This narrative of the witnesses that the accused, despite observant of the fact that the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9), for the first time in their lives, were sleeping inside the house where the occurrence took place, still committed the same, is atypical and cannot be believed. It is opposed to human conduct that an assailant would, despite having the knowledge of the presence of the witnesses, proceed with the commission of the offence. It is all the more illogical that being perceptive of the chance and rare presence of the witnesses, the accused ran the risk of committing the offence in their presence. Such behavior, on part of the accused, as deposed by the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) runs counter to natural human conduct and behavior. Hence, being perceptive of this strain of human conduct, we are holding that Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) were not present at the time of occurrence, at the place of occurrence and had not witnessed the occurrence. 15. Another aspect of the case noted by us with some gravity is the fact that both the prosecution witnesses namely Abdul Majeed (PW-8) and Hameed Ashraf (PW-9) admitted that the occurrence had taken place Murder Reference No. 10 of 2019 Crl. The law on the burden of proof, as provided in Article 117 of the Qanun-e-Shahadat, 1984, mandates the prosecution to prove, and that too, beyond any doubt, the guilt of the accused for the commission of the crime for which he is charged. The said provision provides: “117. Burden of proof:- Whoever desires any Court to give judgment as to any legal right or liability dependent on the existence of facts which he asserts, must prove that those facts exist. When a person is bound to prove the existence of any fact, it is said that the burden of proof lies on that person.” On a conceptual plain, Article 117 of the Qanun-e-Shahadat, 1984 enshrines the foundational principle of our criminal justice system, whereby the accused is presumed to be innocent unless proved otherwise. Accordingly, the burden is placed on the prosecution to prove beyond Murder Reference No. 10 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 353-J of 2019 34 doubt the guilt of the accused which burden can never be shifted to the accused, unless the legislature by express terms commands otherwise.

It is only when the prosecution is able to discharge the burden of proof by establishing the elements of the offence, which are sufficient to bring home the guilt of the accused then, the burden is shifted upon the accused, inter alia, under Article 122 of the Qanun-e-Shahadat, 1984, to produce evidence of facts, which are especially in his exclusive knowledge, and practically impossible for the prosecution to prove, to avoid conviction. Article 122 reads as under: “122. Burden of proving fact especially within knowledge:- When any fact is especially within the knowledge of any person, the burden to proving that fact is upon him.” It has to be kept in mind that Article 122 of the Qanun-e-Shahadat, 1984 comes into play only when the prosecution has proved the guilt of the accused by producing sufficient evidence, except the facts referred in Article 122 Qanun-e-Shahadat, 1984, leading to the inescapable conclusion that the offence was committed by the accused. Then, the burden is on the accused not to prove his innocence, but only to produce evidence enough to create doubts in the prosecution's case. The article 122 of the Qanun-e-Shahadat, 1984 cannot be used to undermine the well-established rule of law that, save in a very exceptional class of case, the burden is on the prosecution and never shifts. Throughout the web of the Law one golden thread is always to be seen, that it is the duty of the prosecution to prove the accused's guilt subject to any statutory exception. No matter what the charge, the principle that the prosecution must prove the guilt of the accused is the law and no attempt to whittle it down can be entertained. If there is circumstance which created reasonable doubt in a prudent mind about the guilt of the accused, then the accused would be entitled to the benefit of doubt not as a matter of grace and concession but as a matter of right.” 25. For what has been discussed above Criminal Appeal No.353-J of 2019 lodged by Abdul Rehman son of Ahmad Din (appellant) is allowed and the conviction and sentence of the appellant awarded by the learned trial court through the impugned judgment dated 27.05.2019 are hereby set-aside. Abdul Rehman son of Ahmad Din (appellant) is ordered to be acquitted by extending him the benefit of doubt. Abdul Rehman son of Ahmad Din (appellant) is in custody and is directed to be released forthwith if not required in any other case. 26. Murder Reference No.10 of 2019 is answered in Negative and the death sentence awarded to Abdul Rehman son of Ahmad Din is Not Confirmed.
